name: Great Pyramid of Giza
significance: Last surviving Wonder of the Ancient World, built ~2560 BCE. Engineering marvel demonstrating advanced mathematics and construction techniques of ancient Egypt.

name: CERN - Large Hadron Collider
significance: World's largest and most powerful particle accelerator. Site of the Higgs boson discovery in 2012, advancing our understanding of fundamental physics.

name: Colosseum
significance: Largest ancient amphitheatre ever built (70-80 CE). Icon of Roman engineering, architecture, and the sociopolitical power of the Roman Empire.

name: Machu Picchu
significance: 15th-century Inca citadel at 2,430m altitude. Masterpiece of engineering, astronomy, and agricultural terracing in extreme terrain.

name: NASA Kennedy Space Center
significance: Primary launch site for NASA's human spaceflight programs since 1968, including Apollo Moon missions and Space Shuttle launches.

name: Stonehenge
significance: Prehistoric monument built ~3000-2000 BCE. Aligned with solstices, demonstrating early astronomical knowledge and Neolithic engineering.

name: Acropolis of Athens
significance: Ancient citadel containing the Parthenon. Birthplace of democracy, Western philosophy, and classical architecture (5th century BCE).

name: Galápagos Islands
significance: Charles Darwin's observations here led to the theory of evolution by natural selection. A living laboratory of unique endemic species.

name: Hiroshima Peace Memorial (Genbaku Dome)
significance: Preserved ruin near the hypocentre of the first atomic bomb attack (1945). Symbol of nuclear devastation and the global peace movement.

name: Library of Alexandria (Bibliotheca Alexandrina)
significance: Modern revival of the ancient Great Library, once the largest repository of knowledge in the classical world. Centre of Hellenistic scholarship.

name: Pompeii
significance: Roman city preserved by the eruption of Vesuvius in 79 CE. Unparalleled archaeological snapshot of daily Roman life, architecture, and urban planning.

name: Chernobyl Exclusion Zone
significance: Site of the 1986 nuclear disaster, the worst in history. Now a study site for radiation ecology, nuclear safety, and nature reclaiming human spaces.

name: Jantar Mantar
significance: 18th-century astronomical observation site with the world's largest stone sundial. Built by Maharaja Jai Singh II to measure celestial positions.

name: Great Wall of China
significance: Series of fortifications built over centuries starting 7th century BCE. Largest military structure ever built, spanning thousands of kilometres.

name: Trinity Test Site
significance: Site of the first nuclear weapon detonation on 16 July 1945. Marked the dawn of the atomic age and transformed geopolitics forever.

name: Petra
significance: Ancient Nabataean city carved into rose-red rock cliffs (~300 BCE). Engineering feat with advanced water conduit systems in an arid desert.

name: Olduvai Gorge
significance: One of the most important paleoanthropological sites. Early human fossils and stone tools spanning 2 million years of hominin evolution discovered here.

name: Royal Greenwich Observatory
significance: Home of the Prime Meridian (0° longitude) and Greenwich Mean Time. Foundational to global navigation, timekeeping, and cartography.

name: Angkor Wat
significance: Largest religious monument in the world, built in the 12th century. Demonstrates Khmer hydraulic engineering and astronomical alignment.

name: Mohenjo-daro
significance: Major centre of the Indus Valley Civilisation (~2500 BCE). One of the world's earliest major cities with advanced urban planning, drainage, and sanitation.

name: Deutsches Museum
significance: World's largest museum of science and technology. Houses original artefacts including early physics apparatus, aviation pioneers' machines, and mining tech.

name: Easter Island (Rapa Nui)
significance: Home to nearly 1,000 monolithic Moai statues. A case study in Polynesian navigation, megalithic engineering, and ecological collapse.

name: Darwin's Down House
significance: Charles Darwin's home for 40 years where he wrote 'On the Origin of Species' and conducted crucial experiments on evolution.

name: Taj Mahal
significance: Mughal architectural masterpiece completed in 1653. Demonstrates advanced symmetry, optical illusion techniques, and material science with inlaid gemstones.

name: Arecibo Observatory (ruins)
significance: Once the world's largest single-aperture radio telescope (1963-2020). Key to discoveries in pulsars, asteroids, and SETI research.

name: Valley of the Kings
significance: Royal burial ground for pharaohs of the New Kingdom (1539-1075 BCE). Tutankhamun's tomb discovered here in 1922, revolutionising Egyptology.

name: Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum
significance: Houses the Wright Flyer, Apollo 11 command module, and Spirit of St. Louis. Comprehensive history of human flight and space exploration.

name: Göbekli Tepe
significance: World's oldest known megalithic temple (~9500 BCE). Predates agriculture, challenging theories about the origins of civilisation and religion.

name: Alhambra
significance: Moorish palace-fortress showcasing Islamic art, geometry, and hydraulic engineering. Peak of Nasrid dynasty's mathematical and artistic achievements.

name: Nalanda University Ruins
significance: Ancient centre of learning (5th-12th century CE) attracting scholars from across Asia. One of the world's first residential universities with advanced curricula.

name: Teotihuacan
significance: Pre-Columbian city with the Pyramid of the Sun, one of the largest structures in the ancient Americas. Advanced urban planning and astronomical alignment.

name: Marie Curie's Laboratory (Musée Curie)
significance: Where Marie and Pierre Curie conducted groundbreaking research on radioactivity. Marie Curie was the first person to win two Nobel Prizes.

name: Terracotta Army
significance: Thousands of life-size clay soldiers buried with Emperor Qin Shi Huang (~210 BCE). Reveals ancient Chinese metallurgy, mass production techniques, and funerary art.

name: Bletchley Park
significance: WWII codebreaking centre where Alan Turing cracked the Enigma code. Birthplace of modern computing and signals intelligence.

name: Hagia Sophia
significance: Built 537 CE with the world's largest dome for nearly a millennium. Masterpiece of Byzantine architecture, engineering, and religious history.

name: Chichén Itzá
significance: Major Mayan city with El Castillo pyramid demonstrating precise astronomical knowledge — the serpent shadow appears during equinoxes.

name: Los Alamos National Laboratory
significance: Birthplace of the Manhattan Project and the atomic bomb. Continues as a premier research facility for nuclear science, computing, and national security.

name: Lascaux Caves
significance: Paleolithic cave paintings (~17,000 years old). Among the earliest known examples of human art, depicting animals and possible astronomical maps.

name: Konark Sun Temple
significance: 13th-century temple shaped as a giant chariot with 24 carved wheels functioning as sundials. Demonstrates advanced Indian astronomy and stone engineering.

name: Dead Sea Scrolls Caves (Qumran)
significance: Discovery site of the oldest known manuscripts of the Hebrew Bible (dating 3rd century BCE to 1st century CE). Transformed biblical scholarship.

name: Svalbard Global Seed Vault
significance: Stores duplicate seed samples from worldwide gene banks as insurance against catastrophic loss of crop diversity. Humanity's agricultural backup.

name: Persepolis
significance: Ceremonial capital of the Achaemenid Empire (~515 BCE). Showcases the engineering and artistry of the largest empire of the ancient world.

name: Einstein House
significance: Where Albert Einstein lived in 1903-1905 and developed his theory of special relativity and the photoelectric effect during his 'miracle year'.

name: Robben Island
significance: Prison where Nelson Mandela was held for 18 years. Symbol of the struggle against apartheid and the triumph of human rights.

name: Hampi
significance: Ruins of Vijayanagara, one of the richest and largest cities in the world during the 14th-16th centuries. Advanced water management and Dravidian architecture.

name: Auschwitz-Birkenau
significance: Largest Nazi concentration and extermination camp. Over 1.1 million people perished here. Essential memorial to the Holocaust and human rights.

name: Atacama Large Millimeter Array (ALMA)
significance: Most powerful radio telescope array in the world at 5,000m altitude. Captures images of planet-forming discs and the earliest galaxies.

name: Parthenon (within Acropolis)
significance: Temple dedicated to Athena (447-432 BCE). Mathematical perfection in its proportions using entasis and optical corrections influenced architecture globally.

name: Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL)
significance: NASA's centre for robotic space exploration. Built and operates Mars rovers, Voyager probes, and numerous deep-space missions.

name: Baikonur Cosmodrome
significance: World's first and largest space launch facility. Launch site of Sputnik (1957) and Yuri Gagarin's historic first human spaceflight (1961).

name: Akihabara Electric Town
significance: Global epicentre of anime, manga, and otaku culture. Dense with multi-storey shops, maid cafés, and arcades that define modern Japanese pop culture exports.

name: Hollywood Sign & Walk of Fame
significance: The iconic sign (erected 1923) and the Walk of Fame with 2,700+ stars define the global film industry's cultural capital and celebrity culture.

name: Ghibli Museum
significance: Museum dedicated to Studio Ghibli and Hayao Miyazaki's works. Showcases the animation process behind globally beloved films like Spirited Away and My Neighbor Totoro.

name: Skywalker Ranch
significance: George Lucas's filmmaking compound where Star Wars, Indiana Jones, and pioneering VFX/sound design at Industrial Light & Magic and Skywalker Sound were developed.

name: Tezuka Osamu Manga Museum
significance: Dedicated to Osamu Tezuka, the 'God of Manga' who created Astro Boy and pioneered modern anime/manga storytelling conventions used industry-wide today.

name: Weta Workshop & Wētā FX
significance: Visual effects and prop studio behind Lord of the Rings, Avatar, and Planet of the Apes. Revolutionised motion capture, miniatures, and digital VFX in cinema.

name: Pinewood Studios
significance: Filming location of James Bond, Star Wars sequels, Marvel MCU, and Harry Potter. One of the most important film studios in cinema history since 1936.

name: Nakano Broadway
significance: Multi-level shopping complex known as the hardcore otaku alternative to Akihabara. Hub for rare anime collectibles, vintage manga, and Mandarake stores.

name: Vasquez Rocks Natural Area
significance: Iconic sci-fi filming location used in Star Trek (the Gorn fight), Westworld, Power Rangers, and dozens of sci-fi films. Its alien-like rock formations became synonymous with extraterrestrial landscapes.

name: Toei Animation Museum
significance: Museum at the studio behind Dragon Ball, One Piece, Sailor Moon, and Slam Dunk. Toei shaped the global anime industry and popularised the shōnen genre worldwide.

name: Ramoji Film City
significance: Guinness World Record holder as the largest film studio complex in the world (674 hectares). Major production hub for Tollywood, Bollywood, and multilingual Indian cinema.

name: Film City (Dadasaheb Phalke Chitranagari)
significance: Sprawling studio complex in Goregaon where countless Bollywood films are shot. Named after Dadasaheb Phalke, the father of Indian cinema who made India's first feature film in 1913.

name: AVM Studios
significance: India's oldest surviving film studio (established 1946). The beating heart of Kollywood and Tamil cinema, producing over 150 films including early talkies that shaped South Indian film culture.

name: Prasad IMAX & Film Laboratory
significance: Home to one of the world's largest IMAX screens and the Prasad Film Labs which processed prints for a massive share of Indian films. A landmark of Telugu and pan-Indian cinema infrastructure.

name: National Museum of Indian Cinema
significance: India's first national museum dedicated to cinema, tracing the journey from Dadasaheb Phalke's first silent film to modern blockbusters. Houses rare memorabilia, vintage cameras, and interactive exhibits on Indian filmmaking history.
